results.title: Abercrombie shares jump 13% on earnings beat even as Iran conflict hits sales
results.description: Abercrombie & Fitch posted mixed first-quarter results on Wednesday and weaker-than-expected guidance after the conflict in the Middle East “directly impacted” sales, the company said. Despite those challenges, shares jumped about 13% in morning trading as the company easily topped Wall Street’s earnings estimates. Sales in Abercrombie’s Europe, Middle East and Africa region fell 10% [...] The post Abercrombie shares jump 13% on earnings beat even as Iran conflict hits sales appeared first on NYT News Today .

results.title: Cambodia sentences six for murder of Korean student
results.description: PHNOM PENH - ⁠A ⁠Cambodian court has convicted ​and sentenced six Chinese nationals to life in prison on ​charges they tortured and ‌murdered a South Korean student involved with one of Cambodia’s notorious scam centres, a court spokesperson said on Wednesday.

results.title: 13% energy price hike makes ‘workers pay for Trump’s war’
results.description: HOUSEHOLDS must not bear the brunt of US President Donald Trump’s illegal war, campaigners warned today, as energy bills are set to rise by 13 per cent. Ofgem’s new price cap from July 1 will see the typical household face an annual energy cost of £1,862 — up £221 on current levels, or £18 a month. The regulator said higher wholesale gas prices, “driven by ongoing conflict in the Middle East,” were behind the increase. Energy costs have soared since the US and Israel began their war on Iran and the subsequent blocking of the Strait of Hormuz.

results.title: Kenney Shields Park to get new, temporary basketball courts
results.description: Kenney Shields Park in Covington will be getting temporary (but long-term) basketball courts following a vote Tuesday night from the Covington Board of Commissioners. The commissioners also voted to create a temporary walking path to augment the new courts. results.title: West Asia Crisis: ‘Reported Iran MoU is a complete fabrication,’ White House refutes Iranian state TV report
results.description: The White House dismissed Iranian state media reports of a US-Iran draft MoU as fabricated after Iranian TV claimed the proposed agreement included lifting naval restrictions and restoring Strait of Hormuz traffic.
